diagrams.description: This timeline traces the evolution of Pedagogy from ancient sages to modern educational science. Key milestones: ancient philosophical teaching (Socrates’ maieutics, Confucius’ moral cultivation); Comenius’s 'Didactica Magna' (1657) advocating universal education; Rousseau’s 'Emile' (1762) emphasizing natural development; Pestalozzi’s child‑centered methods (early 1800s); Herbart’s systematic instruction (1806); the progressive education movement (Dewey, 1897); Montessori’s scientific pedagogy (1907); Piaget’s cognitive development theory (1936) and Vygotsky’s sociocultural theory (1934, translated 1960s); Freire’s 'Pedagogy of the Oppressed' (1968) and critical pedagogy; the rise of constructivist and digital pedagogies (1980s‑present); and 21st‑century innovations like flipped classrooms, personalized learning, and inclusive education.
